title: malty light british turkey neck steamed pudding
description: a hearty and energizing steamed pudding made with turkey neck, suitable for pescatarian diets and served as a traditional british dessert
instructions: in a large mixing bowl, combine self-raising flour, caster sugar, and bread crumbs.
add in butter, whisking until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
add in milk, whisking until a thick batter forms.
add in egg, pickle juice, flaked malted barley, and malt extract, whisking until well combined.
add in turkey neck, making sure it is fully coated in the batter.
transfer the mixture to a greased pudding basin or a heatproof container.
cover the pudding with parchment paper or a clean tea towel, securing with string if necessary.
place the pudding in a large saucepan, adding enough boiling water to come halfway up the side of the pudding basin.
bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cook for 2 hours, or until the pudding is cooked through and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
remove the pudding from the water and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
